'''''UFC 264: Poirier vs. McGregor 3''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on July 10, 2021 at the [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas–Paradise, NV MSA|Las Vegas Metropolitan Area]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/04/ufc-264-poirier-vs-mcgregor-3-official-t-mobile-arena-las-vegas-full-crowd|title='UFC 264: Poirier vs. McGregor 3' official for T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas with full crowd|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2021-04-14|access-date=2021-04-14}}</ref>

==Background==
[[File:T Mobile Arena The Strip Las Vegas (29798246202).jpg|thumb|left|300px|Due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]], the UFC has been hosting most of its events in [[Las Vegas]] at the [[UFC Apex]] facility, all of them [[Behind closed doors (sport)|behind closed doors]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/05/nevada-athletic-commission-approves-las-vegas-ufc-events-covid-19-coronavirus|title=Nevada Athletic Commission approves first Las Vegas UFC events since COVID-19 outbreak|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2020-05-27|access-date=2020-05-27}}</ref> This was the first time since [[UFC 248]] in March 2020 that [[T-Mobile Arena]] hosted an event, with a full capacity crowd attending it.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/4/14/22383434/ufc-264-takes-place-in-las-vegas-at-full-capacity-dustin-poirier-conor-mcgregor-3-official|title=UFC 264 set for Las Vegas 'at full capacity,' Dustin Poirier vs. Conor McGregor 3 official|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2021-04-14|access-date=2021-04-14}}</ref>]]

A lightweight trilogy between former interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Dustin Poirier]] and former [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight]] and Lightweight Champion [[Conor McGregor]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Dustin-Poirier-vs-Conor-McGregor-3-Set-for-UFC-264-Headliner-on-July-10-178566|title=Dustin Poirier vs. Conor McGregor 3 set for UFC 264 headliner on July 10|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Jay Pettry|date=2021-04-02|access-date=2021-04-02}}</ref> Their first fight was a featherweight bout that happened in September 2014 at [[UFC 178]], where McGregor won by first-round technical knockout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2014/09/ufc-178-results-photos-conor-mcgregor-stops-dustin-poirier-in-first-round|title=UFC 178 results, photos: Conor McGregor stops Dustin Poirier in first round|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2014-09-27|access-date=2021-04-02}}</ref> They then ascended to lightweight for an eventual rematch in January 2021 at [[UFC 257]], where Poirier prevailed via second-round knockout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://edition.cnn.com/2021/01/24/sport/conor-mcgregor-dustin-poirier-match/index.html|title=Dustin Poirier defeats Conor McGregor with a knockout in the 2nd round|publisher=CNN|author=Christina Maxouris|date=2021-01-25|access-date=2021-04-03}}</ref> During fight week, it was revealed that former lightweight champion [[Rafael dos Anjos]] was selected by the organization to serve as a potential replacement in case of need.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://ge.globo.com/combate/noticia/ufc-264-rafael-dos-anjos-e-escalado-como-atleta-substituto-da-luta-entre-mcgregor-e-poirier.ghtml|title=UFC 264: Rafael dos Anjos is named replacement athlete in McGregor-Poirier fight|publisher=ge.globo.com|author=Evelyn Rodrigues|date=2021-07-08|access-date=2021-07-08}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between former [[Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki#Welterweight championship|KSW Welterweight Champion]] and [[Dricus du Plessis]] and [[Trevin Giles]] was scheduled to take place at [[UFC on ESPN: Brunson vs. Holland]], but du Plessis was pulled from the event due to [[travel visa|visa]] issues which restricted his travel.<ref name="Dolidze In">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/3/10/22323869/dricus-du-plessis-out-of-ufc-vegas-22-roman-dolidze-steps-in-to-fight-trevin-giles|title=Dricus Du Plessis out of UFC Vegas 22, Roman Dolidze steps in to fight Trevin Giles|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee and Mike Heck|date=2021-03-10|access-date=2021-03-10}}</ref> The pairing was rescheduled and took place at this event.<ref name="tryagain">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/04/ufc-264-dricus-du-plessis-vs-trevin-giles-conor-mcgregor-dustin-poirier|title=UFC 264 adds Dricus Du Plessis vs. Trevin Giles to lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2021-04-22|access-date=2021-04-22}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[Sean Brady (fighter)|Sean Brady]] and former interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] challenger [[Kevin Lee (fighter)|Kevin Lee]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.mmaweekly.com/kevin-lee-goes-back-up-to-welterweight-faces-sean-brady-at-ufc-264|title=Kevin Lee goes back up to Welterweight, faces Sean Brady at UFC 264|publisher=mmaweekly.com|author=Hunter Brownstein|date=2021-06-01|access-date=2021-06-24}}</ref> However, Lee withdrew due to injury and the bout was removed from the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/6/24/22549216/kevin-lee-injured-out-of-upcoming-fight-against-sean-brady-at-ufc-264|title= Kevin Lee injured, out of upcoming fight against Sean Brady at UFC 264|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin and Mike Heck|date=2021-06-24|access-date=2021-06-24}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between [[Sean O'Malley (fighter)|Sean O'Malley]] and [[Louis Smolka]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/05/ufc-264-sean-omalley-vs-louis-smolka-bantamwight-added-las-vegas|title=Sean O'Malley vs. Louis Smolka added to UFC 264 in Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2021-05-03|access-date=2021-06-29}}</ref> However on June 29, Smolka withdrew due to an undisclosed injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/6/29/22555952/louis-smolka-out-ufc-264-fight-sean-omalley|title=Louis Smolka out of UFC 264 fight with Sean O'Malley|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2021-06-29|access-date=2021-06-29}}</ref> He was replaced by promotional newcomer Kris Moutinho.<ref name="Krisin">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/06/ufc-264-sean-omalley-to-fight-replacement-kris-moutinho|title=Sean O'Malley sweepstakes over: Newcomer Kris Moutinho gets UFC 264 call|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2021-06-30|access-date=2021-06-30}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, [[Irene Aldana]] weighed in at 139.5 pounds, three and a half pounds over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. Her bout proceeded at catchweight and she was fined 30% of her purse, which went to her opponent [[Yana Kunitskaya]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/07/ufc-264-dustin-poirier-conor-mcgregor-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream|title=UFC 264 weigh-in results: One miss on main card, but full lineup set |publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2021-07-09|access-date=2021-07-09}}</ref>

On July 9, it was announced that the UFC would be increasing bonuses (Fight of the Night and Performance of the Night) from $50,000 to $75,000 for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/07/ufc-264-fight-night-bonuses-set-to-increase-from-50000-to-75000|title=UFC 264 fight night bonuses set to increase from $50,000 to $75,000|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells|date=2021-07-09|access-date=2021-07-10}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[Alen Amedovski]] and [[Hu Yaozong]] was scheduled to be the first preliminary bout of the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://asianmma.com/hu-yaozong-faces-alen-amedovski-at-ufc-264/|title=Hu Yaozong faces Alen Amedovski at UFC 264|publisher=asianmma.com|author=Staff|date=2021-05-22|access-date=2021-07-10}}</ref> However, it was cancelled just hours before taking place due to [[COVID-19]] protocol issues in Amedovski's camp.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/31795790/ufc-middleweight-fight-yu-haozong-alen-amedovski-due-covid-19-protocols|title=Yaozong-Amedovski UFC bout off due to protocols|work=espn.com|author=Marc Raimondi|date=2021-07-10|access-date=2021-07-10}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $75,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/7/11/22572029/ufc-264-bonuses-kris-moutinho-earns-share-of-fight-of-the-night-for-gutsy-performance|title=UFC 264 bonuses: Kris Moutinho earns share of Fight of the Night for gutsy performance|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2021-07-11|access-date=2021-07-11}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Sean O'Malley vs. Kris Moutinho'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Tai Tuivasa''' and '''Dricus du Plessis'''
